extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won the sprint in Sevilla after his Quick-Step team controlled the peloton and caught the breakaway with 7km remaining.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step hunted the break solo without help from other sprint teams, forcing them to spend early for TRENTIN's third win.
FRAILE Omar abandoned during mid-stage despite winning two mountain classifications, signaling deeper form issues not visible in recent climbs.
DE MARCHI Alessandro survived solo longer than teammate DE GENDT after 40km chase began, suggesting superior diesel engine for long escapes.

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: third stage win despite difficult sprint setup shows superior positioning skills when leadout isn't perfect
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: contributed to stage win as part of dominant Quick-Step train, building form after early-race success
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: mentioned alongside LAMPAERT as Quick-Step stage winner earlier in race, team operating with multiple cards
rider_signals.MOSCON Gianni: neutral: second place finish in sprint shows he can follow fast wheels but lacks punch to come around
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: sixth in sprint suggests trying to bank bonus seconds rather than true sprint speed
rider_signals.FRAILE Omar: negative: abandoning mid-flat stage after two mountain classification wins indicates serious underlying illness or injury masked by adrenaline
rider_signals.DE MARCHI Alessandro: positive: strongest of four-man break, resisted until 7km showing diesel endurance that could work in longer mountain breakaways
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: relaxed stage after previous day's troubles suggests team managing stress rather than attacking with 59-second lead
